name: rust
description: Idiomatic Rust conventions, error handling, testing, async, and tooling for production code

decisions.decision: Always use the latest stable Rust edition; set rust-version (MSRV) in Cargo.toml
decisions.rationale: Each edition unlocks safety improvements and language features. The latest edition (currently 2024) adds critical safety defaults like unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn warnings. MSRV communicates the minimum compiler version to downstream users.
decisions.details: Set edition in workspace Cargo.toml. Migrate with cargo fix --edition. Set rust-version to the minimum Rust version your code compiles on. CI should test on both stable and MSRV.

decisions.decision: Use thiserror for library error types, anyhow for application error propagation; never use unwrap() in production code paths
decisions.rationale: thiserror provides zero-cost derive macros for std::error::Error without appearing in your public API. anyhow provides easy context addition with .context(). unwrap() panics on failure — use ? to propagate or expect() with a reason when panic is justified.
decisions.details:
Libraries: define domain-specific error enums with #[derive(thiserror::Error)]. Use #[from] for automatic conversion, #[source] for chains. Applications: use anyhow::Result with .context() for human-readable error chains. For large projects, consider snafu for richer context selectors. For CLIs/dev tools, miette provides diagnostic-quality error output with source code snippets.

Enable clippy::unwrap_used as a lint in production crates, allow in test modules. If you must panic, use expect("reason") with a clear explanation.

decisions.decision: Configure lints in Cargo.toml with workspace inheritance; enable pedantic and nursery with selective allows
decisions.rationale: Cargo.toml lint configuration (RFC 3389) is the modern approach — workspace inheritance ensures consistency across all crates. Pedantic catches real issues but has noisy lints that should be selectively allowed. Restriction group should never be enabled wholesale — cherry-pick specific lints.

decisions.decision: Use Cargo workspaces with crates/ directory; separate lib.rs from main.rs even in binaries
decisions.rationale: Workspaces provide dependency and lint inheritance. Separating lib.rs from main.rs enables unit testing without running the binary and allows library reuse.
decisions.details:
Workspace layout:
  Cargo.toml — [workspace] root with [workspace.dependencies] and [workspace.lints]
  crates/core/ — shared types and traits
  crates/cli/ — binary crate
  crates/server/ — binary crate

For simple projects, single crate with lib.rs + main.rs is fine. main.rs should be thin — parse args, wire deps, call into lib.

Use workspace = true inheritance for all shared deps, package metadata, and lints. Set publish = false for internal workspace crates. Commit Cargo.lock for binaries, not for libraries.

decisions.decision: Multi-layered testing with cargo-nextest as runner; property testing with proptest; snapshot testing with insta
decisions.rationale: cargo-nextest is up to 3x faster than cargo test with per-test process isolation and flaky test detection. proptest catches edge cases manual tests miss. insta makes golden-file testing trivial with cargo insta review.
decisions.details:
Testing pyramid:
1. Unit tests — inline #[cfg(test)] modules with #[test] functions.
2. Property-based — proptest for core logic and algorithms.
3. Snapshot — insta for serialization, CLI output, rendering.
4. Integration — tests/ directory for system-level behavior.
5. Doc tests — runnable examples in doc comments.

Use rstest for parameterized tests and fixtures. Use mockall for trait mocking when needed. Target 80%+ coverage with cargo-llvm-cov.

CI: cargo nextest run --workspace. Coverage: cargo llvm-cov --workspace.

decisions.decision: Use tokio as the async runtime; keep domain logic synchronous; async only at I/O boundaries
decisions.rationale: Tokio is the dominant async runtime (20,000+ crates depend on it). async-std is discontinued (March 2025). Async adds complexity — only use it when you need high-concurrency I/O. Keeping domain logic synchronous makes it testable and composable.
decisions.details:
Use #[tokio::main] for applications. Use tokio::spawn_blocking for CPU-intensive work within async context. Use .instrument(span) from tracing to maintain context across async boundaries.

Prefer the multi-threaded runtime for most applications. Use current_thread for constrained environments.

Anti-patterns: blocking in async code (use spawn_blocking), making everything async when threads would be simpler, forgetting Send + 'static bounds when spawning tasks.

decisions.decision: Use tracing for all logging and observability; never use println! or the log crate directly
decisions.rationale: tracing is the de facto standard for Rust logging, developed by the Tokio team. It supports structured logging, spans, async context propagation, and OpenTelemetry integration. It supersedes the log crate.
decisions.details:
Use tracing for instrumentation. Use tracing-subscriber for configuration (fmt layer, JSON output, EnvFilter for RUST_LOG). Use tracing-log as a compatibility bridge for dependencies still using the log crate.

Structured fields: tracing::info!(user_id = %id, "processing request") — never string interpolation in messages.

Use .instrument(span) on futures for async context. Integrate with tracing-opentelemetry for distributed tracing in production.

decisions.decision: Avoid writing unsafe code; when necessary, minimize scope, document safety invariants, and test with Miri
decisions.rationale: Most unsafe functionality exists in the standard library or established crates. Unsafe blocks that lack safety documentation are time bombs. Miri detects undefined behavior that tests and sanitizers miss.
decisions.details:
Rules:
- Prefer safe abstractions (Rc, Arc, RefCell, Mutex, Pin) over raw pointers.
- Minimize unsafe blocks — one operation per block.
- Every unsafe block needs a // SAFETY: comment explaining why the operation is safe.
- Wrap unsafe code in modules that expose only safe interfaces.
- Run cargo +nightly miri test to detect UB (aliasing violations, data races, use-after-free).
- Enable clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks lint.
- The latest edition defaults unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn to warn — forces explicit unsafe blocks inside unsafe functions.

decisions.decision: All public API items must have doc comments with examples; use deny(missing_docs) in library crates
decisions.rationale: Rust doc comments are runnable tests — they serve as both documentation and regression tests. The Rust API Guidelines require documentation with examples for all public items.
decisions.details:
Structure: one-line summary, detailed explanation, # Examples (runnable), # Errors, # Panics, # Safety (for unsafe fns).

Use /// for item docs, //! for module/crate-level docs. Examples should use ? not unwrap(). Run cargo doc --no-deps --open to review.

Set #![warn(missing_docs)] or #![deny(missing_docs)] in library crate roots. Doc tests run with cargo test --doc.

decisions.decision: Use workspace dependency inheritance; run cargo-deny for license compliance and vulnerability scanning
decisions.rationale: Workspace inheritance eliminates version duplication across crates. cargo-deny subsumes cargo-audit and adds license, ban, and source checks — critical for production software.
decisions.details:
Define shared deps once in [workspace.dependencies] with version and features. Members reference with dep.workspace = true.

Use SemVer ranges ("1.2" not "=1.2.3") to allow compatible updates. Commit Cargo.lock for binaries.

cargo-deny configuration (deny.toml): check advisories, licenses, bans, and sources. Run in CI on every PR. Also run cargo audit on a weekly cron as belt-and-suspenders.

Use cargo-semver-checks before publishing libraries to detect breaking API changes.

decisions.decision: Use rustfmt with default settings; enforce in CI with cargo fmt --check
decisions.rationale: rustfmt eliminates all formatting debates. Default settings match community convention. Consistent formatting reduces review noise.
decisions.details: Run cargo fmt --all -- --check in CI (non-zero exit on diff). Customize minimally via rustfmt.toml if needed — defaults are well-chosen. Use #[rustfmt::skip] for exceptional blocks.

decisions.decision: Profile before optimizing; use criterion or divan for benchmarks; track regressions in CI
decisions.rationale: Micro-benchmarks can be misleading — always profile realistic workloads first. criterion provides statistical analysis with historical tracking. divan is the modern alternative with simpler API and less timing noise in CI.
decisions.details:
Use criterion for comprehensive benchmarking with HTML reports. Use divan for CI benchmarks (less timing noise). Use iai-callgrind for deterministic instruction-count benchmarks.

Use black_box to prevent compiler optimizations in benchmarks. Profile with flamegraph or cargo-instruments before optimizing.

Performance-related crates: rayon (data parallelism), dashmap (concurrent map), smallvec (stack-allocated small vectors), parking_lot (faster locks).
